title: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for creators, businesses, and nonprofits.
h1: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact: A Detailed Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Constant Contact serve different audiences in the email marketing space. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators and course sellers with creator-first features, while Constant Contact focuses on small businesses and nonprofits seeking simplicity. Choosing between them depends on your business model and specific feature needs.
choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller needing creator-specific tools and automations
You want to try the platform free with up to 1,000 subscribers before paying
Visual automation workflows and advanced segmentation are important to your marketing
choose_b_if: You need the lowest entry price and prefer a straightforward, simple platform
You're a nonprofit or local business looking for event management and social media ad integration
SMS marketing capabilities are essential for your communication strategy

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit is better for creators and course sellers with superior automation and landing pages, while Constant Contact is better for nonprofits and local businesses seeking affordability and SMS capabilities. ConvertKit rates higher on G2 (4.4 vs 4.0) and offers a free tier, making it more accessible for startups.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact is cheaper at the entry point, starting at $12/month with no free tier. ConvertKit's paid plans start at $29/month, but it offers a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers, which can be cost-effective for growing creators who don't need to pay immediately.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email list imports and exports. Most data including subscribers and basic information can be migrated, though you may need to reconfigure automations and templates. Both offer customer support to assist with the migration process.
